messages.author: Fidonet127
messages.text: I finally have some good antennas. I went camping at First landing this weekend, companions, room server and a repeater. I wasn’t able to test range much but did do some learning. I was able to reach to my repeater at my tent site to the beach. No contacts while traveling.
messages.content_html: I finally have some good antennas. I went camping at First landing this weekend, companions, room server and a repeater. I wasn’t able to test range much but did do some learning. I was able to reach to my repeater at my tent site to the beach. No contacts while traveling.

messages.author: Fidonet127
Fidonet127
messages.text: I finally put in a permanent repeater setup. Heltec v3, up on 1 1/2” conduit, some sort of waterproof case. I have a car battery in the basement, and using landscape 16 gauge wire to go between. Then using dc to usb-c charger to power the node. Not efficient for power, but I didn’t want to go through all the solar setup with how cold and hot it gets here. Hopefully this lasts through the winter, gain experience and hopefully more people in the area. Also using LMR 400 with N connectors.
messages.content_html: I finally put in a permanent repeater setup. Heltec v3, up on 1 1/2” conduit, some sort of waterproof case. I have a car battery in the basement, and using landscape 16 gauge wire to go between. Then using dc to usb-c charger to power the node. Not efficient for power, but I didn’t want to go through all the solar setup with how cold and hot it gets here. Hopefully this lasts through the winter, gain experience and hopefully more people in the area. Also using LMR 400 with N connectors.

messages.author: Fidonet127
messages.text: Testing so far is not much range. Two problems I think. One is it is about 7.1 meters above ground. Could be more, but I can’t measure accurately. I think the trees are too much interference. Two, I think I need to be near 0.8 to 1 Watt range to help out. Disappointing.
messages.content_html: Testing so far is not much range. Two problems I think. One is it is about 7.1 meters above ground. Could be more, but I can’t measure accurately. I think the trees are too much interference. Two, I think I need to be near 0.8 to 1 Watt range to help out. Disappointing.

messages.author: UP
messages.text: LoRa tends to be rather much line of sight in my experience. From my home without a good placement I was getting about 300m range. My friend had the expensive gear, but lived in a geographic hole for a bit, and just couldn't get signals in or out at all. Getting a node located on top of a big hill really opens everything up, even with the most rudamentry equipment, based on my experience with meshtastic. Making sure to get actual dipoles for antenna helps, but given that it's on a rooftop mast, you could add a ground plane to compensate if you're working with one that's not. I'm in Georgia, but have aspirations to help get the southeast linked up. Good luck!
messages.content_html: LoRa tends to be rather much line of sight in my experience. From my home without a good placement I was getting about 300m range. My friend had the expensive gear, but lived in a geographic hole for a bit, and just couldn't get signals in or out at all. Getting a node located on top of a big hill really opens everything up, even with the most rudamentry equipment, based on my experience with meshtastic. Making sure to get actual dipoles for antenna helps, but given that it's on a rooftop mast, you could add a ground plane to compensate if you're working with one that's not. I'm in Georgia, but have aspirations to help get the southeast linked up. Good luck!
messages.reactions_html: 1

LoRa tends to be rather much line of sight in my experience. From my home without a good placement I was getting about 300m range. My friend had the expensive gear, but lived in a geographic hole for a bit, and just couldn't get signals in or out at all. Getting a node located on top of a big hill really opens everything up, even with the most rudamentry equipment, based on my experience with meshtastic. Making sure to get actual dipoles for antenna helps, but given that it's on a rooftop mast, you could add a ground plane to compensate if you're working with one that's not. I'm in Georgia, but have aspirations to help get the southeast linked up. Good luck!

messages.author: UP
messages.text: That depends on which style of internal construction it is, and most sellers we can readily get antennas from are sadly not motivated to provide detailed or accurate information about that kind of thing. I don't supose your supplier offered detailed datasheets? Those pricy long fiberglass ones are usually dipoles, though, so I'd probably work on slingshotting a cord through the top of a tree to hoist a node up, and analyzing the surrounding topography for the ideal spots to deploy solar. Also, forgot to mention, I love the username.

messages.author: UP
messages.text: Sweet! I hear gopher and fidonet are still kicking. I didn't experience them in their hayday, but with what I've seen happening to the web lately, I'm starting to think maybe those are worth checking out now. This is way off topic, but if you care to DM or take that to the OT channel, I'd love to hear more about it.
messages.content_html: Sweet! I hear gopher and fidonet are still kicking. I didn't experience them in their hayday, but with what I've seen happening to the web lately, I'm starting to think maybe those are worth checking out now. This is way off topic, but if you care to DM or take that to the OT channel, I'd love to hear more about it.
messages.reply_html:
Fidonet127
I used to be a SysOp for a BBS on FidoNet 1:127:? during the modem days. Thus the id. No one has ever says anything about it before.

messages.author: UP
messages.text: I'm also wondering if a southeast thread is a good idea, for more early coordination and then for long range colabs once it takes off. I saw a Tennessee thread, and a Texas group that is using an alternate frequency to be compatible with existing RF filters on meshtastic nodes. There's not much going on yet in the whole region, tho hopefully I'll get a mountain repeater and room server up in the next few days. Trying to get those dots on the map, so people know there's something to try.

messages.author: Fidonet127
messages.text: Playing around at Kings Dominion. I have my carpeater in the parking lot. Due to the hill and obstructions my V3 can only bounce off the carpeater around Main Street area. I was able to bounce it off when at the top of the Ferris wheel. My v3 only has the stock antenna. Somewhere I lost my other pig tail, so I ordered more. Went to the observation deck and that worked well also. Would love to put a repeater up there. (edited)

messages.author: Fidonet127
messages.text: The app has a IAD region that encompasses most of Northern Virginia. That allows us to move around in the region to test mesh coverage. Actual MC coverage in the region is sparse. The app doesn’t allow Wardriving in areas not within at least one region.
messages.content_html: The app has a IAD region that encompasses most of Northern Virginia. That allows us to move around in the region to test mesh coverage. Actual MC coverage in the region is sparse. The app doesn’t allow Wardriving in areas not within at least one region.
